reviews.title: Disappointing experience as Marriott Elite Gold Member
reviews.text:
- When we called customer service to enquire about our room and the person I talked to was very rude, unaccommodating, and gave us blatantly false information (she mentioned that the swimming pools were open)
- In the lobby, the men's washroom flooded and the entire lobby smelled like human feces, it was extremely disgusting and was right next to the communal microwave in which we used to warm up our infant son's formula
- The sliding bathroom door in our room did not close and kept sliding open while in use and we had to use towels to jimmy the door closed
- The elevators were extremely slow and we would often wait 10-15 minutes just to get on
- When I ordered room service, I only wanted a bottle of beer and a tea and the wait was over 1 hour. Absolutely unacceptable.
- This is a review from an Gold Elite Member.

reviews.title: Was a bit of a letdown for a “Mariott”
reviews.text:
Cons:
* Provisions are lacking like had to ask for dental kit bcos it wasnt automatically provided, no water bottles provided either (for comparison Best Western actually provides both)
* No in-room slippers or bathrobes :(
* Sofa upholstery is ripped and you can see the innnards of the sofa Lol
* They have godawful uncomfortable sofas! Seriously. The backrest is ruler straight and exactly 90degrees. Their lounge downstairs also has the same sofa.. Defeats the purpose of a sofa and of a lounge if you ask me.

Pros
* When we landed, we were famished and tired. So we ordered room service. Korean chicken and mushroom soup were fantastic!
* Staff were nice 
* Bath products were provided and the scent is nice
* Bed was soft and had lotsa pillows
* Starbucks was right in the lobby
* They had a viewing deck of the falls, just a plain deck with no chairs or anything. Had bits of grass here and there. Nothing fancy, it was just a deck.
* Maybe about 5min walk from the falls if you take the rail up and down, day pass for it is $9 per adult
* Lots of eating places within walking distance 

Would I book again? Not for us, sorry.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: The view from this hotel is truly outstanding and is the main reason to stay here. The falls-facing rooms offer a spectacular, unobstructed view that is difficult to beat, especially both during the day and at night. That part of the experience absolutely lives up to expectations.
reviews.negatives:
The furniture and overall room décor feel dated and in need of refurbishment, which does not match the premium pricing or the quality one would expect from a Marriott-branded hotel in this location.

Another recurring issue was the elevator wait times. The lines were consistently long, particularly during peak hours, leading to significant delays getting to and from the room.

More concerning was the condition of the parking lot during winter. It was poorly maintained and inadequately salted.

reviews.title: Not worth the money
reviews.text:
The Marriott name is well recognized but the Marriott on the Falls is sub- standard according to the industry. 
- First, there is no breakfast included ( not like Hampton Inn)
- Second, there is no microwave in the room which is  a necessity these days (there is only one microwave to use in the lobby for the whole hotel!)
- Third, there is no coffee or hot water in the lobby for the guests to enjoy during the day or night 
- Fourth (and this is a killer), you have to pay $45CDN plus tax which came to about $55CDN to park for one night! … Imagine you stay for a few days and that’s an extra $110-$165CND out of your pocket.
I would not recommend this hotel although it’s a good location.
reviews.room_type: Cityview, Guest room, 2 Queen, City view

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.positives: Just the location, but not a good stuff not a good furniture so old
reviews.negatives:
I recently stayed at the Marriott Hotel in Niagara Falls after booking through Booking.com, and I was very disappointed with my experience. I paid over $1,000 per night, yet the room did not reflect the value of the price. The couch was broken, there was no water provided in the fridge, and breakfast was not included despite the high rate.

On top of that, I had to pay extra for parking, but even then it was difficult to find a spot, especially if arriving later in the evening. When I raised my concerns with staff at the reception, I did not feel that my concerns were handled with care.

Overall, it was a very frustrating experience and not at all what I expected from a Marriott property. I would not return to this hotel, and I cannot recommend it to others.

reviews.title: Beautiful views, but they nickel and dime.
reviews.positives: During our stay we enjoyed the view of the falls, the proximity to downtown and being able to walk to the casino or clifton hills, we also enjoyed the food at milestones on the falls.
reviews.negatives: To start, parking is $49 dollars a day and you have to cross a busy street to get to the non valet lot. Then, there was no working ice machine on ANY floor! We asked them where we could get ice. We were told they had "no idea" where a working ice machine was. Then, on our booking it was stated we had 2 free breakfasts and any additional guests would have to purchase a breakfast. When we went to get breakfast we were told it wasn't included in our room rate and that we could purchase a breakfast package for a little over $40 A PERSON!! But whatever we considered that a booking.com issue and moved along. We were also told wifi was included, only to see it's actually $15.95 a day. We discussed this again with staff who told us it was a hold charge to ensure the card was still working and would be released 3-7 business days after our stay. Which brings me to my next and most important point. the amount of "hold charges" to make sure "my card is active" for a room I have already paid for is insane and intense. Throughout our stay we received almost $180 in "holds" over a two night stay. Some for as much as $42 and as little as $10 (USD, so more in CAD). When discussing these charges at check out the front desk was rude, dismissive, and spoke down to us simply for inquiring what these charges were for. She told us "it's a hold, you know what a hold is right? it'll be released in 7 business days." we then said "what are the charges FOR, we understand what a hold is but what is each amount for? on the invoice it states hold and nothing else" she stated they were holds to make sure we could pay (we paid in full already), that we still would be charged $90 in parking (that we paid for already and tried to explain this) and that they would be released and waved us off with her hand and said have a good one and turned around to end the conversation.

reviews.positives: View of the falls and comfy bed
reviews.negatives: The heater made noise every time it turned on. Not enough outlets beside the beds. The lighting was poor and couldn’t take pics inside our own room. The barn door looked like it was recently taking from a farm. The shower knob couldn’t push in or pull out to initiate overhead shower feature. The shower gel was basically empty on the first night we arrived. The hot water temperature wasn’t hot enough for me to shave with comfortably. The was no exhaust fan in the washroom to remove smells or steam. The fridge inside the room was Luke warm when we arrived and we couldn’t find the temperature adjustment dial anywhere in the fridge and somehow it turned itself on in the middle of the night and started getting cooler. The initial entrance to the hotel lobby was met with 10 kids running around screaming and a huge lineup to wait on elevators. The hallway by our room was riddled with popcorn and a bottle and can for most of the morning when we left our room to start our day. Parking was $50/ night and it wasn’t even underground or covered parking. The pool was  unaccessible do to large amounts of kids running around in the pool area. It was the worse hotel experience I have ever had.

reviews.title: Scenic Stay with a View at Niagara Falls Marriott
reviews.text:
Stayed at Niagara Falls Marriott on the Falls and overall, it was a memorable experience—especially because of the incredible views.

The highlight of the stay is definitely the location. The hotel sits right along Fallsview Boulevard, and if you book a falls-view room, you’ll wake up to a stunning, almost surreal view of Niagara Falls. It’s also within walking distance to major attractions, which made getting around very convenient. 

Overall, Niagara Falls Marriott on the Falls is a great choice if your priority is the view and location. It may not be the most modern hotel, but the scenery alone makes the stay worth it—perfect for a short getaway or special occasion.

reviews.title: Better than expected
reviews.text:
We only stayed 2 nights to see the Falls. We chose November as there were other places we needed to go as well. The key items for a hotel stay are 1) Cleanliness, 2) WiFi availability, 3) Service levels.

Have to say Marriott on the Falls is outstanding in all three areas. Other plus points was added requests for a kettle which was attended to promptly. A big shout out to Sarah at the front desk as we checked in. Very helpful and knowledgeable of everything that's happening. Also a shout out to Mary Jane who served us at the breakfast.

Cons: I only wish for the lights in the room were brighter. 

Well done Marriott on the Falls! Keep it up

reviews.title: Beautiful window view but there were smudges
reviews.positives: The view is wonderful. We essentially paid for the view. When we needed the cot, they brought it up. The people who work here are helpful.
reviews.negatives:
This hotel was decent. While the view was beautiful, window was very dirty and smudges take away a bit. We have stayed at the other Marriott across the street in the past, which we have enjoyed much more. I should have paid the extra money to go back there.

For a family of 5 (which I booked for) we only had two queen beds, no pull out sofa, which is usually what we have for a family of 5. They did bring up a cot, but almost charged extra for it. 

Bathroom door offers no privacy--barely closes; water pressure was not the best. Felt like Steve Martin in "Trains, Planes, and Automobiles" at times.

Restaurant is very pricey for what it is, and only offered a $7.98 credit for eating there, which is not a great savings after the price for each person. Not worth it--should have gone to Ihop.

reviews.title: That awesome view!
reviews.text: I knew beforehand everything will be expensive since the area is a tourist destination, but it was so worth it staying at this hotel just for the view alone. Our room was on the 27th floor falls view, and it made our trip one for the books! I mean, nothing can beat the view of the two falls, we spent our evening just watching them and waiting for the fireworks. Love the staff, especially Ingrid who did her best to setup mine and my sister's room close to the other. We also dined at their in-house restaurant Milestones and our family had a great experience. The location is so convenient, as the WEGO bus has a stop just outside the hotel, and they even gave complimentary passes for the WEGO bus. Not bad even for the ~CAD50/day parking fee (everywhere else in the area charges parking fee, some even higher priced).

The bed broke soon as we sat on it with springs that fell on the floor. We exchanged rooms shortly after and the staff were quick to move us but didn't offer any consolation. 
We visited the hotel pool and it was run amuck with unsupervised children (a group of 6 or so)  and my wife was hit in the head by a sharp toy they brought with them. We asked where their parents were and ofc they were upstairs and then they left shortly after; thankfully my wife wasn't permanently injured. 
The food was the most bland diner food you could find for both breakfast and dinner. They water down the alcoholic drinks significantly and thats the standard policy it seems as when I asked for a stronger cocktail they say i have to pay additional when you cant taste ANY alcohol in the sangria they made or the aperol spritz my wife ordered. I had to order another shot to add to the drinks for it to be a remotely pleasant cocktail.
